25. Multi Linear Regression Feature Selection Practice(3) for Machine Learning

06 January 2021 - 58 mins read time
Tags: Fast Campus ML Fast Campus ML practice

Source: Fast Campus

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split


# Read Data
data = pd.read_csv("./data/toyotacorolla.csv")
data.head()
Id Model Price Age_08_04 Mfg_Month Mfg_Year KM Fuel_Type HP Met_Color ... Central_Lock Powered_Windows Power_Steering Radio Mistlamps Sport_Model Backseat_Divider Metallic_Rim Radio_cassette Tow_Bar
0 1 TOYOTA Corolla 2.0 D4D HATCHB TERRA 2/3-Doors 13500 23 10 2002 46986 Diesel 90 1 ... 1 1 1 0 0 0 1 0 0 0
1 2 TOYOTA Corolla 2.0 D4D HATCHB TERRA 2/3-Doors 13750 23 10 2002 72937 Diesel 90 1 ... 1 0 1 0 0 0 1 0 0 0
2 3 ?TOYOTA Corolla 2.0 D4D HATCHB TERRA 2/3-Doors 13950 24 9 2002 41711 Diesel 90 1 ... 0 0 1 0 0 0 1 0 0 0
3 4 TOYOTA Corolla 2.0 D4D HATCHB TERRA 2/3-Doors 14950 26 7 2002 48000 Diesel 90 0 ... 0 0 1 0 0 0 1 0 0 0
4 5 TOYOTA Corolla 2.0 D4D HATCHB SOL 2/3-Doors 13750 30 3 2002 38500 Diesel 90 0 ... 1 1 1 0 1 0 1 0 0 0

5 rows × 37 columns

# Shape of Data
rows = data.shape[0]
columns = data.shape[1]
print("cars: {}, features: {}".format(rows, columns))

cars: 1436, features: 37

Feature Engineering

# Find Dummy Variables
data['Fuel_Type'].unique()

array(['Diesel', 'Petrol', 'CNG'], dtype=object)

# Create new Columns to make Dummy to Numerical
dummy_d = np.repeat(0, rows)
dummy_p = np.repeat(0, rows)
dummy_c = np.repeat(0, rows)
dummy_d

array([0, 0, 0, ..., 0, 0, 0])

d_index = np.array(data["Fuel_Type"] == "Diesel")
p_index = np.array(data["Fuel_Type"] == "Petro")
c_index = np.array(data["Fuel_Type"] == "CNG")

d_index

array([ True,  True,  True, ..., False, False, False])

dummy_d[d_index] = 1
dummy_p[p_index] = 1
dummy_c[c_index] = 1

dummy_d

array([1, 1, 1, ..., 0, 0, 0])

# Add dummies
new_data = pd.DataFrame({'Diesel':dummy_d, 'Petro':dummy_p, 'CNG':dummy_c})
data = pd.concat((data,new_data), 1)

data.head()

5 rows × 40 columns

# drop useless columns
data = data.drop(['Id', 'Model', 'Fuel_Type'], axis=1)

data.head()

5 rows × 37 columns

data = sm.add_constant(data,has_constant='add')
data.head()
const Price Age_08_04 Mfg_Month Mfg_Year KM HP Met_Color Automatic cc ... Radio Mistlamps Sport_Model Backseat_Divider Metallic_Rim Radio_cassette Tow_Bar Diesel Petro CNG

5 rows × 38 columns

Test and Train Data Split

feature_columns = list(data.columns.difference(['Price']))
X = data[feature_columns]
y = data.Price
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y)

print(X_train.shape, X_test.shape, y_test.shape, y_train.shape)

(1077, 37) (359, 37) (359,) (1077,)

Fitting Linear Model

model = sm.OLS(y_train, X_train)
model = model.fit()

model.summary()

Using VIF, Check Multicollinearity

from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor
vif = pd.DataFrame()
vif['VIF Factor'] = [variance_inflation_factor(data.values, i)
                     for i in range(data.shape[1])]
vif["Features"] = data.columns
vif

/Users/joonholee/opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.8/site-packages/statsmodels/regression/linear_model.py:1685: RuntimeWarning: divide by zero encountered in double_scalars

# Check Residual Using QQ-plot
residual = model.resid
fig = sm.qqplot(residual, fit=True, line='45')


# Residual Pattern Check For Train Data
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
y_pred = model.predict(X_train)
fig = plt.scatter(y_pred, residual, s=3)
plt.xlim(4000,30000)
plt.xlabel('Fitted Values')
plt.ylabel('Residual')

Text(0, 0.5, 'Residual')


# Prediction For Test Data
y_pred_2 = model.predict(X_test)

# Residual Plot for Test Data
plt.plot(np.array(y_test - y_pred_2), label="Prediction for Test")
plt.ylim(-12000, 6000)
plt.legend()
plt.show()


# Mean Squared Error
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
mean_squared_error(y_true = y_test, y_pred = y_pred_2)

1302808.6100696896

Feature Selection Using AIC

# Function that fits model and return AIC
def processSubset(X, y, feature_set):
    model = sm.OLS(y, X[list(feature_set)]) # modeling
    model = model.fit() # fitting
    AIC = model.aic # AIC
    return {'model':model, 'AIC':AIC}

print(processSubset(X=X_train, y=y_train, feature_set=feature_columns))

{'model': <statsmodels.regression.linear_model.RegressionResultsWrapper object at 0x7ff39de5d1c0>, 'AIC': 18203.1686998716}

import time
import itertools
# Function that returns best model(lowest AIC)
def getBest(X,y,k):
    tic = time.time() # Starting Time
    results = []
    for combo in itertools.combinations(X.columns.difference(['const']), k):
        combo = (list(combo)+['const'])
        results.append(processSubset(X,y,feature_set=combo))
    models = pd.DataFrame(results)
    best_model = models.loc[models['AIC'].argmin()]
    toc = time.time()
    print("Processed", models.shape[0], "Models on", k, "Predictors in",
          (toc - tic), "Seconds")
    return best_model

print(getBest(X=X_train, y=y_train,k=2))

Processed 630 Models on 2 Predictors in 0.7494561672210693 Seconds
model    <statsmodels.regression.linear_model.Regressio...

tic = time.time()
for i in range(1,4):
    models.loc[i] = getBest(X=X_train, y=y_train, k=i)
toc = time.time()
print("Total elapsed time: ", (toc-tic),"seconds")

Using Feedforward and AIC

#function that shows feedforward
def forward(X, y, predictors):
    # Check whether data variables are in pre-defined predictors.
    remaining_predictors = [p for p in X.columns.difference(['const']) if p not in predictors]
    tic = time.time()
    results = []
    for p in remaining_predictors:
        results.append(processSubset(X=X, y= y, feature_set=predictors+[p]+['const']))
    # Transfer to Data Frame
    models = pd.DataFrame(results)

    # Select the minimum AIC model
    best_model = models.loc[models['AIC'].argmin()] # index
    toc = time.time()
    print("Processed ", models.shape[0], "models on", len(predictors)+1, "predictors in", (toc-tic))
    print('Selected predictors:',best_model['model'].model.exog_names,' AIC:',best_model[0] )
    return best_model

# feedforward model

def forward_model(X,y):
    Fmodels = pd.DataFrame(columns=["AIC", "model"])
    tic = time.time()
    # pre-defined variables
    predictors = []
    # variables 1~10 : 0~9 -> 1~10
    for i in range(1, len(X.columns.difference(['const'])) + 1):
        Forward_result = forward(X=X,y=y,predictors=predictors)
        if i > 1:
            if Forward_result['AIC'] > Fmodel_before:
                break
        Fmodels.loc[i] = Forward_result
        predictors = Fmodels.loc[i]["model"].model.exog_names
        Fmodel_before = Fmodels.loc[i]["AIC"]
        predictors = [ k for k in predictors if k != 'const']
    toc = time.time()
    print("Total elapsed time:", (toc - tic), "seconds.")

    return(Fmodels['model'][len(Fmodels['model'])])

Forward_best_model = forward_model(X=X_train, y= y_train)

Forward_best_model.aic

18184.21667252312

Backward Elimination and AIC

# backward elimination
def backward(X,y,predictors):
    tic = time.time()
    results = []
    for combo in itertools.combinations(predictors, len(predictors) - 1):
        results.append(processSubset(X=X, y= y,feature_set=list(combo)+['const']))
    models = pd.DataFrame(results)
    # select the best model(lowest AIC)
    best_model = models.loc[models['AIC'].argmin()]
    toc = time.time()
    print("Processed ", models.shape[0], "models on", len(predictors) - 1, "predictors in",
          (toc - tic))
    print('Selected predictors:',best_model['model'].model.exog_names,' AIC:',best_model[0] )
    return best_model

# backward elimination model
def backward_model(X, y):
    Bmodels = pd.DataFrame(columns=["AIC", "model"], index = range(1,len(X.columns)))
    tic = time.time()
    predictors = X.columns.difference(['const'])
    Bmodel_before = processSubset(X,y,predictors)['AIC']
    while (len(predictors) > 1):
        Backward_result = backward(X=X_train, y= y_train, predictors = predictors)
        if Backward_result['AIC'] > Bmodel_before:
            break
        Bmodels.loc[len(predictors) - 1] = Backward_result
        predictors = Bmodels.loc[len(predictors) - 1]["model"].model.exog_names
        Bmodel_before = Backward_result['AIC']
        predictors = [ k for k in predictors if k != 'const']

    toc = time.time()
    print("Total elapsed time:", (toc - tic), "seconds.")
    return (Bmodels['model'].dropna().iloc[0])

Backward_best_model = backward_model(X=X_train,y=y_train)

Backward_best_model.aic

18184.216672523162

Stepwise and AIC

def Stepwise_model(X,y):
    Stepmodels = pd.DataFrame(columns=["AIC", "model"])
    tic = time.time()
    predictors = []
    Smodel_before = processSubset(X,y,predictors+['const'])['AIC']
    # variables 1~10 : 0~9 -> 1~10
    for i in range(1, len(X.columns.difference(['const'])) + 1):
        Forward_result = forward(X=X, y=y, predictors=predictors) # constant added
        print('forward')
        Stepmodels.loc[i] = Forward_result
        predictors = Stepmodels.loc[i]["model"].model.exog_names
        predictors = [ k for k in predictors if k != 'const']
        Backward_result = backward(X=X, y=y, predictors=predictors)
        if Backward_result['AIC']< Forward_result['AIC']:
            Stepmodels.loc[i] = Backward_result
            predictors = Stepmodels.loc[i]["model"].model.exog_names
            Smodel_before = Stepmodels.loc[i]["AIC"]
            predictors = [ k for k in predictors if k != 'const']
            print('backward')
        if Stepmodels.loc[i]['AIC']> Smodel_before:
            break
        else:
            Smodel_before = Stepmodels.loc[i]["AIC"]
    toc = time.time()
    print("Total elapsed time:", (toc - tic), "seconds.")
    return (Stepmodels['model'][len(Stepmodels['model'])])

Stepwise_best_model=Stepwise_model(X=X_train,y=y_train)
